context: Run cell C flags (fixed 8x2, adamw_torch_fused, GC on) on the FULL training dataset (1.04M samples after speaker-disjoint split) with --dataloader_num_workers 2 and --max_steps 100, no --max_audio_secs filter, no page-cache warming. Compare to grid cell C (subset 5000, num_workers=4) to isolate the num_workers=2 + cold-full-dataset deltas.
verdict_reason: mean_gpu_util (steady, 20s offset from training start) = 45.67% < 55% acceptable floor. +4.67 pp over grid cell C (41.0%) but within measurement noise (grid repro hit 50.57%). Not a high-enough signal to upgrade from blocked.

findings.num_workers_2_vs_4: num_workers=2 is marginally better than the grid's num_workers=4 on the 4-core cgroup (+4.7 pp util, -0.16 s/step). Consistent with 'fewer worker processes reduce context-switch thrash on the CPU quota'. Worth keeping as a default.
findings.cold_vs_warm_cache: Cold-cache full-dataset performs within measurement noise of warm-cache 5000-sample subset. This is strong evidence that page-cache hit rate is NOT the binding constraint — otherwise cold full-dataset would be meaningfully worse.
findings.decode_bound_not_io_bound: The bottleneck is CPU-bound audio decode (MP3 decompress + resample + mel-spectrogram), not filesystem read latency. 4-core cgroup can decode ~6-8 samples/sec total, which matches the GPU's 16 samples / 2 s = 8 samples/sec demand — the system is decode-starved, not I/O-starved.
findings.remediation_implications: Options 2 (/dev/shm staging) and 3 (page cache warming) are ruled out — they cache bytes, not decoded tensors. Option 1 (WebDataset pre-decode to log-mel shards) and Option 5 (migrate to a host with more CPU cores) are the two viable paths forward.
